data.title: Microservices
data.description: Netflix employs a microservices architecture, which allows for scalability and independent deployment of services.
data.technical_spec: Decomposed system where each service runs a unique process and communicates through APIs.

data.title: Cloud-based Infrastructure
data.description: The platform utilizes AWS for scalable and reliable cloud hosting.
data.technical_spec: Utilization of Amazon Web Services for elastic compute, storage, and database services.

data.title: CDN Integration
data.description: Netflix uses content delivery networks to efficiently stream content globally.
data.technical_spec: Partnership with multiple CDN providers to cache and deliver content closer to users.

data.title: Database Management
data.description: Netflix uses a combination of SQL and NoSQL databases to manage user data and media content.
data.technical_spec: Mix of Cassandra, MySQL, and other database technologies for different data needs.

data.title: Load Balancing
data.description: Smart load balancing mechanisms are in place to manage traffic and ensure availability.
data.technical_spec: Use of custom and AWS Elastic Load Balancing for distributing network loads.
